headline: Production evaluation and agent memory drive a clustered breakout in the technique layer this week.
body: The week's rotation concentrated on the machinery for running agents in production, with multi-step workflows, benchmarks, human-in-the-loop, observability, and RLHF all moving and breaking out together. Meta Superintelligence Labs detailed continuous evaluation pipelines, online versus offline architectures, and drift and hallucination detection for agentic systems, while OpenGov described scaled production deployment across state and local governments using A2A and evaluation frameworks. A parallel thread centered on agent memory and state, with arguments that coding agents fail without persistent context and that durable append-only session logs, not orchestration layers or models, should anchor agent identity. Grounding and primitive-action decomposition entered as new technique breakouts alongside the established AI search and analytics movers.

cited_items.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
